REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT CHARLIE SETO'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO 1) COMPEL ARBITRATION, AND 2) COMPEL PLAINTIFF TO FILE REQUEST FOR DISMISSAL, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR THE COURT TO ORDER DISMISSAL IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF IS IN BREACH OF THE LICENSE AGREEMENT AND SETTLEMENT OF CLAIMS, PARAGRAPH 10, WHICH UNEQUIVOCALLY REQUIRED PLAINTIFF TO DISMISS THE ACTION WITH PREJUDICE WITHIN 10 DAYS OF EXECUTION OF THE AGREEMENT. PLAINTIFF SHALL PREPARE A FORM OF DISMISSAL, PRESENT A HARD COPY TO THE COURT FOR REVIEW AT THE HEARING, AND BE PREPARED TO FILE IT ELECTRONICALLY BY NOON ON JANUARY 31, 2022. PURSUANT TO THE TERMS OF THE AGREEMENT, EXECUTED BY THE PARTIES, THE COURT RETAINS JURISDICTION TO ENFORCE THE AGREEMENT, WHICH INCLUDES A BINDING ARBITRATION CLAUSE COVERING "ANY DISPUTE BETWEEN THE PARTIES ARISING FROM THIS AGREEMENT OF PERFORMANCE UNDER IT." ANY SUCH DISPUTE (INCLUDING THE DISPUTE OVER THE TERMINATION OF THE LICENSE OR EXISTENCE OF AN EASEMENT CLAIMED IN THIS ACTION) SHALL PROCEED IN ARBITRATION. THE COURT WILL THEREAFTER HOLD A HEARING AND ENTER A FINAL JUDGMENT ON ANY MOTION TO CONFIRM/VACATE THE ARBITRATION AWARD PER THE TERMS OF THE AGREEMENT. SUPPLEMENTAL OPPOSITION AND REPLY ARE STRICKEN. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

DEFENDANT CHARLIE SETO's Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award is GRANTED. No opposition filed.

Order and judgment signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)

DEFENDANT CHARLIE SETO'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO 1) COMPEL ARBITRATION, AND 2) COMPEL PLAINTIFF TO FILE REQUEST FOR DISMISSAL, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR THE COURT TO ORDER DISMISSAL
Off Calendar Jan-31-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Gener D. Beintez; Ibarra Professional Law Corporation; 415-398-5329; appearing for defendant Charlie Seto.

No other appearances. Not contested. Prior to the hearing, the court received a courtesy copy of plaintiff's Request for Dismissal. The court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CHARLIE SETO'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO 1) COMPEL ARBITRATION, AND 2) COMPEL PLAINTIFF TO FILE REQUEST FOR DISMISSAL, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR THE COURT TO ORDER DISMISSAL is GRANTED. Plaintiff is in breach of the License Agreement and Settlement of Claims, paragraph 10, which unequivocally required Plaintiff to dismiss the action with prejudice within 10 days of execution of the Agreement. Plaintiff shall prepare a form of dismissal, present a hard copy to the Court for review at the hearing, and be prepared to file it electronically by noon on January 31, 2022. Pursuant to the terms of the Agreement, executed by the parties, the Court retains jurisdiction to enforce the Agreement, which includes a binding Arbitration clause covering "any dispute between the parties arising from this Agreement of performance under it." Any such dispute (including the dispute over the termination of the license or existence of an easement claimed in this action) shall proceed in Arbitration. The Court will thereafter hold a hearing and enter a final judgment on any motion to confirm/vacate the arbitration award per the terms of the Agreement. Supplemental Opposition and Reply are stricken.

Order signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

DEFENDANT CHARLIE SETO'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO 1) COMPEL ARBITRATION, AND 2) COMPEL PLAINTIFF TO FILE REQUEST FOR DISMISSAL, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR THE COURT TO ORDER DISMISSAL
